lessons.title: Java is a high-level, object-oriented programming language
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"developed by James Gosling at Sun Microsystems (now owned by Oracle) in 1995. It is designed to be platform-independent, meaning that Java programs can run on any device that has a Java Virtual Machine (JVM).","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Java is a high-level, object-oriented programming language\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Mastering this concept will significantly improve your technical execution.","practiceTask":"Review Java is a high-level, object-oriented programming language and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}

lessons.title: Key Features of Java
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"Platform Independence – \"Write Once, Run Anywhere\" (WORA). Object-Oriented – Follows OOP principles (Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Abstraction). Simple and Easy to Learn – Syntax is similar to C++ but removes complex features like pointers.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Key Features of Java\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"High Performance – Uses Just-In-Time (JIT) compiler to improve speed. Rich API – Provides a vast set of built-in libraries for various functionalities.","practiceTask":"Review Key Features of Java and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}

title: It Services & It Consulting
description: Comprehensive study material covering It Services & It Consulting.

lessons.title: Java Architecture

lessons.title: // Reading different types of inputs System.out.print("Enter an integer: ");
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"IT SERVICES & IT CONSULTING int num = scanner.nextInt(); System.out.print(\"Enter a double: \"); double decimal = scanner.nextDouble(); scanner.nextLine(); // Consume the newline left-over System.out.print(\"Enter a string: \"); String text = scanner.nextLine(); System.out.println(\"\\nYou entered:\"); System.out.println(\"Integer: \" + num); System.out.println(\"Double: \" + decimal); System.out.println(\"String: \" + text); scanner.close(); // Always close the scanner Advantages: Easy to use for different data types. Supports methods like nextInt(), nextDouble(), nextLine(), Disadvantages: Slightly slower compared to BufferedReader. nextLine() issue after nextInt() or nextDouble() (requires extra scanner.nextLine()).","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: // Reading different types of inputs System.out.print(\"Enter an integer: \");\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Mastering this concept will significantly improve your technical execution.","practiceTask":"Review // Reading different types of inputs System.out.print(\"Enter an integer: \"); and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}

Avoids nextLine() issues. Disadvantages: Requires explicit parsing (Integer.parseInt(), Double.parseDouble()).","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Disadvantages: Requires explicit parsing (Integer.parseInt(), Double.parseDouble()). lessons.title: Method Overriding occurs when a subclass (child class)

title: Encapsulation What is Encapsulation?
description: Comprehensive study material covering Encapsulation What is Encapsulation?.

lessons.title: Introduction & Overview
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"Encapsulation is one of the four fundamental Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) principles in Java, along with Abstraction, Inheritance, and Polymorphism. It is the process of wrapping data (variables) and code (methods) together into a single unit, typically a class.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Introduction & Overview\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Mastering this concept will significantly improve your technical execution.","practiceTask":"Review Introduction & Overview and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}

lessons.title: Encapsulation ensures that the internal details of a class are
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"hidden from the outside world, and access to the data is controlled through getter and setter methods. Key Points of Encapsulation Data Hiding: Restricts direct access to class fields, protecting data from accidental modification. Increased Security: Prevents unauthorized access and modification of data.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Encapsulation ensures that the internal details of a class are\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"How to Achieve Encapsulation in Java? Encapsulation is implemented in Java using the following steps: Declare variables as private (to restrict direct access).1. Provide public getter and setter methods to access and modify the variables.","practiceTask":"Review Encapsulation ensures that the internal details of a class are and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}
